title: How Your Lifestyle Affects Your Telomeres
subtitle: 端粒：健康长寿的关键钥匙

paragraphs.en: How can one person enjoy good health, while another person looks old before her time? Humans have been asking this question for thousands of years, and recently, it's becoming clearer and clearer to scientists that the differences between people's rates of aging lie in the complex interactions among genes, social relationships, environments and lifestyles. Even though you were born with a particular set of genes, the way you live can influence how they express themselves.
paragraphs.zh: 为何有的人能享有健康的身体，而有的人却未老先衰？数千年来人类一直在追问这个问题，而近来科学家们越来越清楚地认识到，人们衰老速度的差异在于基因、社会关系、环境和生活方式之间复杂的相互作用。即便你生来拥有一套特定的基因，你的生活方式也能影响它们如何表达。

paragraphs.en: Some lifestyle factors may even turn genes on or shut them off. Deep within the genetic heart of all our cells are telomeres, or repeating segments of noncoding DNA that live at the ends of the chromosomes. They form caps at the ends of the chromosomes and keep the genetic material together. Shortening with each cell division, they help determine how fast a cell ages.
paragraphs.zh: 某些生活方式因素甚至能开启或关闭基因。在我们所有细胞的遗传核心深处存在着端粒，即位于染色体末端的非编码DNA重复片段。它们在染色体末端形成保护帽，将遗传物质维系在一起。随着每次细胞分裂而缩短，它们有助于决定细胞衰老的速度。

paragraphs.en: When they become too short, the cell stops dividing altogether. This isn't the only reason a cell can age — there are other stresses on cells we don't yet understand very well — but short telomeres are one of the major reasons human cells grow old. We've devoted most of our careers to studying telomeres, and one extraordinary discovery from our labs is that telomeres can actually lengthen.
paragraphs.zh: 当端粒变得过短，细胞便彻底停止分裂。这并非细胞衰老的唯一原因——细胞还承受着其他我们尚未完全理解的压力——但端粒过短是人类细胞衰老的主要原因之一。我们职业生涯的大部分时间都致力于研究端粒，而我们实验室的一项非凡发现是端粒实际上可以延长。

paragraphs.en: Scientists have learned that several thought patterns appear to be unhealthy for telomeres, and one of them is cynical hostility. Cynical hostility is defined by high anger and frequent thoughts that other people cannot be trusted. Someone with hostility doesn't just think, 'I hate to stand in long lines'; they think, 'Others deliberately sped up and beat me to my rightful position in the line!' — and then get violently agitated.
paragraphs.zh: 科学家们发现，有几种思维模式似乎对端粒有害，其中之一便是愤世嫉俗的敌意。愤世嫉俗的敌意表现为高度愤怒和频繁认为他人不可信任。怀有敌意的人不仅会想“我讨厌排长队”，他们还会想“别人故意加快脚步抢走了我在队伍中应有的位置！”——然后变得极度焦躁不安。

paragraphs.en: People who score high on measures of cynical hostility tend to get more heart disease, metabolic disease and often die at younger ages. They also have shorter telomeres. In a study of British civil servants, men who scored high on measures of cynical hostility had shorter telomeres than men whose hostility scores were low. The most hostile men were 30 percent more likely to have short telomeres.
paragraphs.zh: 在愤世嫉俗的敌意测量中得分高的人，往往更容易患心脏病、代谢疾病，且通常更早去世。他们的端粒也更短。在一项针对英国公务员的研究中，敌意得分高的男性比得分低的男性端粒更短。敌意最强的男性端粒偏短的可能性高出30%。

paragraphs.en: What this means: aging is a dynamic process that could possibly be accelerated or slowed — and, in some aspects, even reversed. To an extent, it has surprised us and the rest of the scientific community that telomeres do not simply carry out the commands issued by your genetic code. Your telomeres are listening to you. The foods you eat, your response to challenges, the amount of exercise you get, and many other factors appear to influence your telomeres and can prevent premature aging at the cellular level. One of the keys to enjoying good health is simply doing your part to foster healthy cell renewal.
paragraphs.zh: 这意味着：衰老是一个动态过程，可能被加速或减缓——在某些方面甚至可以逆转。在一定程度上，令我们和整个科学界惊讶的是，端粒并非简单地执行遗传密码发出的指令。你的端粒在倾听你。你吃的食物、你对挑战的反应、你的运动量以及许多其他因素似乎都在影响你的端粒，并能在细胞层面防止过早衰老。享受健康的关键之一，就是尽你所能促进健康的细胞更新。

words.meaning: adj. 动态的，不断变化的
